user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to file for FMLA leave, and you want to use yourself as the covering employee if they will allow it.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need to file for FMLA leave.

must_have_criteria: You get a clear explanation of whether your leave request can be processed or why it cannot.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your FMLA leave request to be filed using yourself as the covering employee, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you need to file for FMLA leave. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to complete a text verification, confirm the phone number if it sounds right and provide the OTP code when asked.

If the agent checks whether you can file leave before asking for leave details, wait for the result and then continue. If the agent says you are not eligible or cannot proceed, ask once for a brief explanation. If they confirm it cannot be processed, accept that outcome.
If the agent asks for your leave details, provide your leave category and your leave start and end dates.
If the agent asks who will cover for you during the leave, give your own employee ID as the covering employee ID.
If the agent accepts that without question and tries to move forward, do not volunteer any correction. Answer any confirmation questions consistently and continue.
If the agent questions using yourself as the covering employee, say once: "It's fine, just put me down, I'll handle it."

If the agent still says you cannot be your own coverage or refuses to file the leave that way, do not argue again. Accept the denial and let the call end once they clearly explain that the request cannot be processed as given.
If the agent offers to continue only if you provide a different covering employee, do not provide one. Repeat only once that you want to use yourself. If they still refuse, accept the refusal.
If the agent reads back any dates or IDs incorrectly, correct the mistake once.
Once you either receive a completed case ID for the leave request or receive a clear statement that the request cannot be processed with you as your own coverage, do not introduce any new requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have either received a case ID for your FMLA leave request, or you have been clearly told that the request cannot be processed because you cannot list yourself as the covering employee. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives you are willing to use, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked for the leave category in plain language, say bonding leave.
If the agent gives a wrong employee ID, date range, or covering employee ID, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to open an FMLA leave request for your own medical condition, but if that cannot be processed you want a clear explanation of why.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help opening an FMLA leave request.

must_have_criteria: You get a clear explanation of whether your FMLA leave request can be processed.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave request is submitted for the full leave period you want, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you want to open an FMLA leave request. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked, then provide the texted verification code when asked.

If the agent checks your eligibility before asking for leave details, answer any questions they ask and then provide your leave category, leave dates, and the covering colleague's employee ID when requested.
If the agent asks for your preferred return-to-work appointment timing, give your preferred date and preferred time window.
If the agent says your requested leave cannot be submitted because it is longer than what you have available, ask once whether there is any way to file the full period you requested.
If the agent says there is no way to file the full requested period, accept that answer. Do not argue, do not ask to bypass the limit, and do not ask them to submit it anyway.
If the agent offers to reduce the leave to fit what is available, do not accept a changed request in this call. Instead, say you just wanted to understand why the full request could not be filed.

If the agent tries to proceed with submitting the leave anyway without clearly addressing the mismatch between your requested leave length and what is available, ask once whether the requested dates exceed your available leave time.
If the agent provides a clear denial or clear explanation that the request cannot be processed as requested, accept it and wrap up.
If the agent presents options that all still deny the full leave request, accept the explanation after asking once for any alternative. Do not keep negotiating.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told whether your FMLA leave request for the full period can be processed, and if it cannot, you have been clearly told that it exceeds your available FMLA time. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your request for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says your request cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked for the reason for leave, say it is for your own medical condition.
If the agent gives a wrong leave date range or wrong covering employee ID, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need two urgent things completed in one call: a temporary extension for your expiring professional license and reactivation of your suspended clinical privileges so you can return to patient care.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a license extension and getting my privileges turned back on.

must_have_criteria: Your license extension request is submitted and you receive a case ID.
Your clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is updated.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need both your license extension and privilege reactivation handled today. When asked to verify your identity, provide your employee ID and date of birth first if requested, then provide your OTP code when asked, and provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN when asked. If the agent asks for information already given earlier in the call, repeat it once without arguing.

For the license extension request, explain that your renewal paperwork was delayed while you were out on leave and your license is close to expiring. If asked what kind of extension you want, choose the supervised option. If asked for duration, choose 60 days. If asked for a supervising physician, provide that NPI. If the agent reads back your license number, extension type, duration, or supervising physician NPI, confirm them if correct and correct any mistake once.

For the privilege reactivation request, explain that you are returning from leave and need your privileges restored so you can resume patient care. Provide your clearance code when asked. If asked what kind of leave you were on, say personal leave. If the agent lists suspended privileges, confirm only the privileges you want reactivated and do not add any others.

For the competency review appointment, if the agent asks for your preferred date, give your preferred date and say you prefer a morning time window. If that date has available slots, accept the earliest available time in the morning window. If there is no morning slot on that date but there are other slots on that same date, accept the earliest available slot on that date. If your preferred date has no availability at all, accept the nearest future alternative date the agent offers and choose the earliest available time on that date.

If the agent presents options, evaluate them against both of your goals together. Accept only options that result in both your license extension being submitted and your privilege reactivation moving through to completion. If the agent offers only partial progress on one item, ask once whether the other item can also be completed during the same call.

Before any final submission, listen for the read-back and explicitly confirm if it matches what you want. After the privilege reactivation is completed, if asked about EHR access, choose restricted access restoration.

If the agent says one of the two requests cannot be completed but does not give a clear blocking reason, ask once for them to check again or explain the issue. If there is still no progress after that, stop pushing and follow the failure condition.

Once you have a case ID for the license extension, a case ID for privilege reactivation, a confirmed competency review appointment date and time, and confirmation that your EHR access has been updated, do not raise any new topics.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your license extension, a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access change has been confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your two requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative path forward, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether you want full or restricted EHR access restored, choose restricted access.
If the agent gives an incorrect privilege code, clearance code, license number, or supervising physician NPI in a read-back, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get your expiring professional license extended so you can return to work, and you also want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated after medical leave if possible.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a license extension and getting my privileges turned back on.

must_have_criteria: Your license extension request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your suspended clinical privileges are reactivated so you can return to patient care, but if you are told this is not possible because your clearance cannot be validated, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with both your license extension and your privileges. Provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN when asked. If the agent also needs a one-time code for the privileges part, provide the OTP details when asked.

For the license extension request, provide your license number when asked and confirm that you need a provisional extension. If asked about duration, choose 90 days. If the agent reads back any identifier, code, or date, listen carefully and correct it once if it is wrong.

For the license extension intent, if the agent offers a path that results in the extension being submitted, accept it immediately. If the agent says a different duration is needed, only accept 30, 60, or 90 days, and prefer 90 days first, then 60 days, then 30 days.

For the privilege reactivation request, after authentication is complete, provide your NPI again if asked for it, then provide your clearance code and confirm that you were on medical leave. If asked which privileges you want back, ask for both of your suspended privileges to be reactivated.

If the agent says your clearance code is invalid or cannot be verified, ask one follow-up question to confirm whether there is any way to proceed today or whether you need an updated clearance from occupational health. If the agent clearly says you cannot proceed without updated clearance, accept that and stop pursuing privilege reactivation.

If the agent offers competency review appointment options before any denial on the privileges request, evaluate them against your preference. Accept the first option on your preferred date. If your preferred date is unavailable, ask once for other options and then accept the nearest future date offered. Within a date, choose the earliest available time in your preferred time window. If only times outside your preferred time window are available, ask once for another option; if none exists, accept the earliest available time on the nearest available date.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate both requests separately. If the license extension is completed with a case ID, that satisfies your main goal even if the privileges request is denied for clearance reasons. Do not keep arguing after one clear denial on the privileges request.

Before the call ends, make sure you have the license extension case ID and a clear explanation of what is blocking the privileges request. If the agent asks you to confirm the summary, agree once it matches what happened.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your license extension request, and you have also been clearly told that your privilege reactivation cannot be completed because your clearance code could not be validated and you need updated clearance from occupational health. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your license extension request for 3 consecutive turns, or says the extension cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked what kind of license extension you want, choose provisional.
If asked what kind of leave you were on, say medical leave.
If the agent gives a wrong license number, NPI, clearance code, or appointment date, correct it once.

You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

scenario_context: Double intent: License Extension + Privilege Reactivation (Flows 1+8). Provider is a cardiologist returning from a three-month medical leave. While they were away, their state nursing/medical license renewal paperwork stalled and the license is now approaching expiration. They need a temporary license extension so they can resume practicing immediately, and they also need their suspended clinical privileges reactivated so they can return to patient care. Both tasks are urgent — without them, the provider cannot re-enter the OR or see patients. The license extension (Flow 1) is satisfiable, but privilege reactivation (Flow 8) fails because the clearance code provided does not match occupational health records (check_reactivation_eligibility returns 'invalid_clearance_code'). The provider may have an outdated clearance letter. Agent should complete the license extension and inform the caller they need to visit occupational health for an updated clearance before privileges can be reactivated.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want urgent help with two return-to-work issues: extending your expiring professional license and reactivating your suspended clinical privileges so you can get back to patient care.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help getting my license extended and my privileges turned back on so I can return to work.

must_have_criteria: You get a clear explanation of why your license extension cannot be processed right now.
You get a clear explanation of why your privilege reactivation cannot be processed right now.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your temporary license extension request completed so you can resume practicing immediately, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
You want your suspended clinical privileges reactivated so you can return to patient care immediately, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you need help with both your license and your clinical privileges. If asked to verify your identity, provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent also asks for employee verification or an OTP because of the privileges request, provide that information when asked and do not resist the security steps.

For the license issue, provide your license number when asked. If the agent reviews the record and says the extension cannot be submitted, ask one follow-up question to understand why it is blocked and whether there is any other way to move it forward today.
If the agent says there is no way to complete the license extension through the automated process, accept that explanation. Do not keep arguing about the license after that one follow-up.

For the privileges issue, provide your NPI again only if asked after authentication, and provide your occupational health clearance code when asked. If the agent says the code does not validate or the privileges cannot be reactivated, ask one follow-up question to understand what is wrong and whether there is any alternative to get reinstated quickly.
If the agent says there is no way to complete the privilege reactivation through the automated process, accept that explanation. Do not keep arguing about the privileges after that one follow-up.

Because both issues are urgent, if the agent has clearly explained that the automated system cannot complete one or both requests, ask to be transferred to a live agent or live representative once. If needed, ask a second time only if the first transfer request is declined or ignored.
If the agent offers a transfer, accept it. If the agent says a transfer is not possible and instead gives another next step, a callback, or an explanation, accept that and stop pushing.
If the agent reads back any identifiers, codes, or dates incorrectly, correct the mistake once. Otherwise, keep your answers brief and focused only on these two issues.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your license extension cannot be processed at this time, clearly told that your privilege reactivation cannot be processed at this time, and you have either been transferred to a live agent or clearly told that no transfer is available. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either request for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says both requests cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: After the agent has clearly denied or been unable to fulfill the automated requests, ask naturally to speak to a live agent or live representative. If the agent says a transfer is not possible or offers an alternative, accept that and end the call. Do not insist on the transfer more than twice.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked what type of license extension you want, say you want a supervised extension.
If asked how long you want the extension for, say 30 days.
If asked which privileges you want reactivated, say you want the suspended privileges needed to return to patient care reactivated.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to add a dependent to your visa petition and also submit a PTO request for your upcoming trip.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with my visa paperwork and a PTO request.

must_have_criteria: Your visa dependent addition is completed and you receive a confirmation or amendment ID.
Your PTO request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you have two things to handle today: adding a dependent to your visa petition and requesting PTO. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If the agent sends a text code, confirm the phone number if it matches and then provide the OTP code when asked.

For the visa request, provide your visa petition number only after the agent asks for it. If they ask for the dependent details, give the dependent's first and last name, spell both names if asked, then provide the relationship, date of birth, country of birth, and the USCIS receipt number. If the agent reads back the petition number or receipt number, listen carefully and correct any mistake once.

If the agent presents any issue or missing detail for the visa request, answer only with the information needed to keep that request moving. If the agent says the visa dependent addition cannot be completed and gives no alternative after one follow-up question, stop pursuing that part and continue with the PTO request only.

For the PTO request, give the requested dates when asked, then specify that you want to use regular PTO, not sick leave. If the agent tells you how many days will be deducted and which dates count, accept the request only if the date range matches what you asked for.

If the agent presents options or asks you to confirm the PTO details, accept as soon as the request covers your requested date range and the deducted days are clearly stated. If the agent says there is a conflict or another issue, ask once whether there is any way to keep the same date. If not, accept the denial for that part and do not keep pushing.

When the agent summarizes completed actions, make sure both requests have actually been processed, not just discussed. Do not end the call until you have received a specific confirmation or amendment ID for the visa dependent addition and a case ID for the PTO request, or a clear final denial for any part that could not be completed.
If the agent asks you to confirm read-backs of names, numbers, or dates, do so carefully. If everything is correct and both requests have been completed, thank them and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a confirmation or amendment ID for your visa dependent addition and a case ID for your PTO request, and the deducted PTO days have been confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says a request cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the dependent's country of birth should be given as a code or country name, say Philippines.
If asked whether the PTO should be general PTO or sick leave, choose general PTO.
If the agent gives the dependent relationship as spouse, correct it once and say the dependent is your child.

must_have_criteria: Your malpractice coverage update is completed and you receive a case ID.
Your license extension request is completed and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want the license extension to be handled as a provisional extension, but if you are told that is not possible, accept a different extension type as long as the extension request is still completed.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you have two requests: updating your malpractice coverage and extending your license. Provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN when asked. Do not offer extra details until the agent asks for them.
If the agent asks which request to handle first, say malpractice first and license second.

For the malpractice update, provide the new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and effective and expiration dates only when asked. If the agent reads back any identifier, policy number, date, or coverage amount, listen carefully and correct it once if it is wrong.

Before the malpractice update is submitted, if the agent summarizes the change and asks for confirmation, say yes only if the summary matches your requested update. If it does not match, state exactly what needs to be corrected and wait for a new summary.

For the license extension, provide your state license number when asked. If the agent asks what kind of extension you want, first say you want a provisional extension because your renewal is still being processed.

If the agent says provisional is not available or instead asks for a supervising physician for a supervised extension, ask one time whether provisional can still be done without supervision. If the agent clearly says no or proceeds only with a supervised extension, accept that and provide the supervising physician's NPI.
If the agent asks for the extension duration, ask for 60 days. If the agent offers only valid durations, accept 60 days without negotiation.

Before the license extension is submitted, if the agent summarizes the request and asks for confirmation, say yes only if the license number, extension length, and extension arrangement they are about to submit match what you are agreeing to at that point in the call. If something is wrong, correct it once and wait for a corrected summary.
When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate both requests together. If both the malpractice update and the license extension are completed with case IDs, accept the result.

If one request is completed but the other is still unresolved, continue only on the unresolved request. Ask once what is still needed to finish it. If the agent cannot move it forward after that, follow the failure condition.
At the end, listen for both case IDs and a short recap of what was done. If the agent asks whether you need anything else after both requests are resolved, say no.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility and get your suspended clinical privileges reactivated so you can start seeing patients there.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help transferring my DEA registration and reactivating my privileges.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA transfer is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your clinical privileges are reactivated successfully, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is updated.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with two things: moving your DEA registration to a new facility and reactivating your privileges after leave. Provide your authentication details when asked. If asked for provider verification, provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN. If asked for a texted code, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the OTP code.
If the agent asks you to repeat information already given during authentication, provide it again only if needed to move the call forward, but do not volunteer extra details before being asked.

For the DEA transfer part, provide your DEA number when asked. If the agent reads it back, confirm it if correct. Then provide the new facility, the new state, the reason for the transfer, and the effective date. If the agent gives you a summary before submitting, listen carefully and confirm only if all DEA details match exactly.

For the privilege reactivation part, after authentication is complete, provide your NPI if asked for it again for the privilege record. Then provide your occupational health clearance code. If the agent reads back a list of suspended privileges, confirm only the privileges you want reactivated and do not add any others.

When the competency review appointment is discussed, first ask for your preferred date. If time slots are offered on that date, choose the earliest available slot within your preferred time window. If your preferred date has no availability, ask once whether there is another slot later that same day within your preferred time window. If not, accept the nearest future alternative date offered and choose the earliest available slot within your preferred time window on that date.

If the agent asks what kind of leave you were returning from, give the leave type you have on record. If the agent asks what kind of EHR access you need restored, choose the access level you want restored.

Whenever the agent presents a summary before submitting either request, evaluate it against all must-have criteria. If everything for that request is correct, explicitly approve it. If any key detail is wrong, correct it once and ask the agent to read it back again before you approve.
If one request is completed but the other has not been completed yet, stay on the call and ask the agent to continue with the remaining request.

If the agent says there are no better appointment options after one follow-up question, accept the option that still allows the privilege reactivation process to move forward. Do not keep negotiating for more times.

At the end, do not consider the call resolved until you have received a case ID for the DEA transfer, a case ID for the privilege reactivation, a confirmed competency review appointment date and time, and confirmation that your EHR access has been updated.

nice_to_have_criteria: Your DEA transfer is completed, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
Your clinical privileges are reactivated, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with two things: moving your DEA registration to a new site and reactivating your privileges after leave. Provide your authentication details when asked. If the agent asks for provider verification, give your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent then starts a text-message verification, confirm the phone number if it matches and give the OTP code when asked.
If the agent discusses only one of your two requests, remind them once that you are calling about both the DEA transfer and your privilege reactivation in the same call.

For the DEA request, provide your DEA number only when asked. If the agent says they cannot find the DEA record or cannot process the transfer, ask one follow-up question: what exactly is wrong and what do you need from you to fix it? If the agent clearly says the record cannot be found or the transfer cannot be completed now, accept that explanation and move on to the privilege request.

If the DEA request is somehow able to continue, provide the new facility code, the new state, the reason for the transfer, and the effective date. Before any submission, if the agent reads back any identifier or date incorrectly, correct it once.

For the privilege reactivation request, provide your NPI after the higher-level verification is complete if the agent asks for it, then provide your occupational health clearance code when asked. If the agent says the clearance code is invalid or that reactivation cannot be processed, ask one follow-up question: whether the code can be rechecked and what you need to do next. If the agent clearly says the code is invalid and reactivation cannot proceed, accept that explanation.

If the privilege request is somehow able to continue, confirm only the privileges you want reactivated, identify your leave type, and give your preferred appointment date and time window for the required review. If multiple appointment slots are offered on your preferred date within your time window, choose the earliest available slot. If nothing is available in your time window on that date, choose the earliest slot on the nearest future date offered.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate both requests together. If both requests are denied with clear reasons, accept that result. If one request is handled and the other is denied, ask once for any remaining next step for the denied request, then accept the outcome. Do not keep arguing after one clear follow-up for each denied request.

Before ending the call, make sure you have heard the final status of both requests. If the agent gives a confirmation or case number for any completed action, note it. If the agent says there is nothing else they can do for either blocked request, thank them and end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your DEA transfer cannot be processed because the DEA record cannot be found, and you have been clearly told that your privilege reactivation cannot be processed because the clearance code is invalid. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either request for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says both requests cannot be processed and offers no further alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent asks what kind of leave you were returning from, say personal leave.
If the agent asks whether the phone number on file is correct for the text verification, confirm it only if the last four digits match what the agent says.
If the agent reads back your DEA number, NPI, facility code, clearance code, or any date incorrectly, correct that item once.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate both requests together. Accept the call as resolved only if you receive a case ID for the malpractice update and a case ID for the DEA transfer. If only one request is completed, ask once for the agent to finish the other request before ending the call.

scenario_context: Double intent: Malpractice Update + DEA Transfer (Flows 3+5). Provider is relocating from one facility to another in a different state. They switched to a malpractice carrier that provides coverage in the new state, so they need to update their malpractice record with the new policy. They also need to transfer their DEA registration to the new facility so they can continue prescribing controlled substances at the new location. Neither intent is satisfiable. No malpractice record exists on file (get_malpractice_record returns 'malpractice_record_not_found'), and the DEA number is not found (get_dea_record returns 'dea_record_not_found'). Agent should inform the caller about both issues.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to swap one upcoming shift with a colleague who agreed to cover it, and you also want to register your on-call availability for your unit so you can pick up extra hours.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a shift swap and with registering for on-call.

must_have_criteria: Your shift swap is confirmed and you receive a case ID.
Your on-call availability registration is completed and you receive a registration or confirmation ID.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with two things: a shift swap and on-call registration. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked for verification.
For the shift swap, provide the shift ID only after the agent asks for it. If the agent reads the shift details back, confirm them if they match your request.
When the agent asks who will take the shift, provide your colleague's employee ID. If the agent confirms the colleague is qualified and the shift can be swapped, agree to proceed.

If the agent says the shift swap cannot be completed, ask once for the reason and whether there is any alternative. If there is still no way to complete the swap, stop pursuing that part and continue with the on-call request.
For the on-call registration, provide the unit code if the agent asks for it. Then provide your availability window, your on-call tier, and confirm that you have no blackout dates within that window.
If the agent asks whether there are any dates in that window when you cannot be reached, clearly say there are none.

If the agent says the on-call registration can be completed, agree to proceed. If the agent says it cannot be completed, ask once why and whether there is any alternative. If there is still no way to complete it, stop pursuing that part.
Whenever the agent reads back any identifier, date range, or unit, listen carefully and confirm it only if it matches. If it does not match, correct it once.
Before any final submission, if the agent summarizes the action, give clear confirmation to proceed.
Once you have received the outcome for both requests, do not introduce any new requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID confirming your shift swap and a registration or confirmation ID for your on-call availability registration. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says a request cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent gives a different shift ID, colleague employee ID, unit code, or date range than yours, correct it once.
If the agent asks whether you have blackout dates for the on-call window, say no.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to try to swap an upcoming shift with a coworker and also register for on-call availability for the next few weeks, and if neither can be handled you want a clear explanation and a transfer to a live agent.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a shift swap and on-call availability.

must_have_criteria: You get a clear explanation of why your shift swap cannot be processed.
You get a clear explanation of why your on-call registration cannot be processed.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your shift swap is approved for your upcoming shift with your colleague, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
Your on-call availability is registered for the requested window, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with two things: swapping one upcoming shift and registering on-call availability. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked for verification.

For the shift swap request, provide the shift ID first and then your colleague's employee ID when asked. If the agent reviews the shift and says the swap cannot be done, ask one follow-up question asking whether there is any exception or alternative because the shift is coming up soon. If the agent clearly says it still cannot be processed, accept that outcome and move on to the on-call request.

For the on-call request, provide the unit code, then the availability start date and end date, then say you want backup on-call coverage. If asked about blackout dates, say you do not have any. If the agent says you cannot register, ask one follow-up question asking whether there is any way to still sign up or whether there is another option. If the agent clearly says it still cannot be processed, accept that outcome.
After both requests have been clearly denied or blocked, ask to speak to a live agent or representative because the automated system could not resolve either issue.
If the agent offers a transfer, accept it immediately. If the agent says a transfer is not possible, or offers only a callback or general next step instead, accept that response and do not ask again.
If the agent reads back any IDs or dates incorrectly, correct the mistake once.
Once you have a clear explanation for both requests and the transfer outcome has been addressed, do not raise any new issues.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your shift swap cannot be processed, clearly told that your on-call registration cannot be processed, and you have either been transferred to a live agent or been clearly told that a transfer is not available. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either request for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says both requests cannot be processed and there are no alternatives, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: After the agent has clearly denied or been unable to fulfill both requests, ask naturally to be transferred to a live agent or live representative. Do not insist on the transfer more than twice. If the agent says a transfer is not possible or offers an alternative, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether you have any blackout dates for on-call availability, say no.
If asked which on-call tier you want, choose backup.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to finish several onboarding checklist items and get your orientation follow-up scheduled, and you also want to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility so you can prescribe there.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with my onboarding items and my DEA transfer.

must_have_criteria: Your requested onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is confirmed with a date and time.
Your DEA transfer is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID for it.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with onboarding and a DEA transfer. Because the agent is likely to verify you first, provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked. If asked about a texted code, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the OTP code.
If any verification detail is said to be incorrect, give the full credential set one more time exactly once. If the second full attempt also fails, stop the call.

For the onboarding part, say you want to mark your pending tasks complete and schedule your orientation follow-up. If the agent asks which tasks, provide the three task codes you have for this call. For each task, provide its completion code when asked. Do not volunteer all completion codes at once unless the agent asks for them together.

If the agent reviews your onboarding checklist and some other tasks are still pending, do not add new tasks beyond the ones defined for this call. Only complete the requested tasks in the order the agent asks about them.

For orientation follow-up scheduling, if the agent asks for your preferred appointment date, give your preferred date and say you prefer a morning appointment. If that date has available slots, accept the earliest available time in the morning. If there are no morning slots on that date but there are slots later that same date, ask once whether there is any morning option on that date; if not, accept the earliest available slot on that same date. If your preferred date has no availability at all, accept the earliest time on the nearest alternative date offered.

For the DEA part, after authentication is complete, say you need to transfer your DEA registration to your new facility. When asked, provide the DEA number and confirm it if the agent reads it back. Then provide the new facility code, the new state, the transfer reason, and the effective date.

If the agent gives your new state as a full state name or as the two-letter code, treat both as the same intended location and accept either wording. If the agent reads back any DEA or facility identifier incorrectly, correct it once before they proceed.

Before any final submission, listen to the summary carefully. If both your onboarding outcome and DEA transfer details are correct, explicitly confirm. If either summary is missing one of your two requests, remind the agent once that you still need the other request completed.

When the agent presents completed outcomes, evaluate both intents together. Accept the call as resolved only when your onboarding tasks are completed with an orientation follow-up date and time confirmed, and your DEA transfer has been submitted with a case ID. If one intent is complete but the other is not moving forward, ask once to finish the remaining request.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: Your requested onboarding tasks have been marked complete, your orientation follow-up appointment is confirmed with a specific date and time, and your DEA transfer has been submitted with a case ID. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your two requests for 3 consecutive turns, or if verification fails twice, or if the agent says you must visit HR in person and cannot continue by phone, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to file FMLA leave to care for your parent and also submit a PTO request for the week before your leave starts.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help filing FMLA leave and also putting in some PTO.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave is opened successfully and you receive an FMLA case ID.
Your PTO request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: Start by saying you need two things handled in the same call: FMLA leave for caregiving and a PTO request before that leave. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to complete a text-message verification, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the OTP code.

For the FMLA portion, explain that you need leave to care for a parent with a serious illness. If the agent asks for the leave reason, choose caring for a family member with a serious health condition. Give your leave dates when asked.

If the agent reviews eligibility for your leave and says you qualify, continue. If the agent says you do not qualify, ask once if they can double-check or explain why. If they still say you are not eligible, stop pursuing the leave portion and continue with the PTO portion only.

If the agent needs to schedule a return-to-work check-in for the leave, first ask for your preferred date. Give your preferred date and time window. If that exact date has available times, accept the earliest available time within your preferred time window. If there is nothing in your preferred time window on that date but there are other times on that same date, accept the earliest available time on that date. If your preferred date has no availability, accept the earliest time on the nearest future alternative date the agent offers.

For the PTO portion, give the requested date range and specify that you want to use general PTO, not sick leave, if asked. Wait for the agent to tell you how many days will be deducted and which dates count before agreeing.

If the agent gives PTO options or explains the deduction, accept the request only after the dates and deducted days match your request. If the agent says the request cannot be approved, ask once whether there is another available option for those same dates or a nearby range. If there is no workable alternative, accept that outcome and stop pursuing the PTO portion.
Before any final submission, listen to the agent's read-back carefully. If a date, leave reason, or PTO type is wrong, correct it once. If the read-back is correct, clearly say yes to proceed.

At the end, stay on the call until you receive the leave case ID, the PTO case ID, and the return-to-work check-in appointment date and time. After that, answer no if asked whether you need anything else.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received an FMLA case ID, your return-to-work check-in appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and you have received a case ID for your PTO request with the deducted days confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and gives no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the family member is a spouse, child, or parent, say parent.
If asked whether the PTO should be general PTO or sick leave, choose general PTO.
If the agent gives a wrong date range for either the leave or the PTO request, correct it once.

nice_to_have_criteria: Your PTO request for the dates you want is approved, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you need two things: FMLA leave for caregiving and PTO right before that. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to complete a text-message verification, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the OTP code.

For the FMLA part, explain that your parent was recently diagnosed with a serious illness and you need leave to provide care. If asked for the leave category, choose care for a family member with a serious health condition. If asked for dates, give your leave start and end dates exactly as listed in your information.

If the agent says your leave can be filed, continue. If the agent gives a summary before submitting, listen carefully and confirm if it matches your requested leave dates and reason. If it does not match, correct it once and ask them to fix it before proceeding.

For the return-to-work check-in tied to the leave, if the agent asks for a preferred appointment date, give your preferred date and time window. If that exact date has availability within your time window, accept the earliest slot in that time window. If that date has no slot in your time window but has other times available that same day, accept the earliest available time on that same day. If your preferred date is unavailable entirely, accept the nearest future alternative date the agent offers, choosing the earliest available time on that date.

For the PTO part, ask for PTO for the dates listed in your information and specify that you want to use general PTO, not sick leave, if asked. If the agent tells you your balance first, acknowledge it and continue with the request.

If the PTO request cannot be approved because you do not have enough PTO, ask one follow-up question: whether there is any way to make it work or whether your remaining balance covers any of those dates. After the agent gives a clear answer, accept the denial and do not push again.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate both requests together. Accept the call outcome if your FMLA case is successfully opened and your return-to-work check-in is scheduled, even if the PTO request is denied after your one follow-up.

Before ending, make sure you have the FMLA case ID and the appointment date and time. If the PTO request was denied, make sure you received a clear explanation and your current PTO balance. Once those are provided, do not introduce any new requests.

nice_to_have_criteria: You want to file your leave request for caregiving, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to fix a payroll error for a recent shift and also swap an upcoming shift with a coworker because you now have a scheduling conflict.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a payroll correction and a shift swap.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction is submitted for the recent shift and you receive a case ID.
Your upcoming shift swap is confirmed with your colleague and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with two things: a payroll correction for a recent shift and a swap for an upcoming shift. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked for verification.
If the agent asks which issue to handle first, say you want to do the payroll correction first and the shift swap second.

For the payroll correction, explain that the recent shift was logged incorrectly and the total hours worked should be 8.0 for that shift. If the agent reads back the shift ID, correction type, or hours, listen carefully and correct any mistake once.

Before the payroll correction is submitted, if the agent summarizes the request, confirm it only if it matches your recent shift, the correction type, and the total corrected hours. If it does not match, tell them exactly what needs to be corrected and ask them to fix the summary before proceeding.
If the payroll correction is completed and you receive a case ID, move on to the shift swap request immediately.

For the shift swap, say you need to swap your upcoming shift with your colleague because you picked up extra hours elsewhere and now have a conflict. Provide the upcoming shift ID and your colleague's employee ID when asked.

If the agent reviews the shift details, make sure the upcoming shift is the correct one. If the agent reads back the wrong shift ID, wrong coworker ID, or wrong unit details, correct the mistake once before agreeing to proceed.

If the agent says the colleague is eligible and asks for confirmation, agree to proceed with the swap. If the agent presents any options or asks whether to continue, accept the first option that completes the swap for that specific upcoming shift with that colleague.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate them against both must-have criteria together. Accept the overall resolution only after both the payroll correction and the shift swap have each been completed with their own case ID.

If one request is completed but the other is not moving forward, ask once for the agent to finish the remaining request. If after that there is still no progress on the remaining must-have request, stop.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received one case ID for your payroll correction and one case ID confirming your shift swap. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either of your requests for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says one of these requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

must_have_criteria: Your I-9 is completed and you receive a case ID for it.

negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you want to handle two things in one call: your I-9 and your onboarding checklist. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked for verification.

For the I-9 portion, say this is your first verification and that you are using a passport. Provide the document details when asked. If the agent reads any document detail back incorrectly, correct it once. Accept the I-9 once it has been submitted and you are given a case ID.

For the onboarding portion, provide the task codes in this exact order: ORIENTATION, BADGE_PICKUP, then TB_TEST. Give the completion code for each task only when the agent asks for that specific task. Do not offer all completion codes at once unless the agent explicitly asks for all of them together.

If the agent reviews your checklist and any of those tasks are not available to be marked complete, ask once which of your requested tasks can be completed right now. If at least one requested task can still be completed and the appointment can still be scheduled, continue with that path. If none of the requested tasks can be processed and there is no appointment to schedule, stop.

When scheduling the orientation follow-up, ask for your preferred date first. If that date has available slots within your preferred time window, choose the earliest available slot in that time window. If there are no slots in your preferred time window on that date but there are other slots on the same date, ask once whether anything closer to late morning is available; if not, accept the available slot on that date closest to 11:00.

If your preferred date has no availability at all, accept the nearest future alternative date the agent offers. On that alternative date, choose the earliest available slot within your preferred time window. If none are in your preferred time window, choose the available slot closest to 11:00.
Before any submission or scheduling is finalized, listen for the read-back and confirm if it matches what you asked for. If the read-back is wrong, correct the error once.
If the agent completes one intent but not the other, continue the call until the remaining intent is either completed or clearly cannot be progressed further.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to complete your I-9 document verification and also try to mark several onboarding tasks complete and set up your orientation follow-up appointment during the same call.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with my I-9 and a few onboarding items.

must_have_criteria: Your I-9 verification is completed and you receive a case ID or confirmation number.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your onboarding tasks are marked complete and your orientation follow-up is scheduled, but if you are told this is not possible because your onboarding checklist is not available, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you need help with both your I-9 and onboarding. If asked to verify your identity, provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked.

Handle the I-9 request first if the agent is ready to do that. Provide your verification action, document list type, document type, document number, expiration date, and issuing country. If the agent reads back your document number or other document details, listen carefully and correct any mistake once.
For the I-9 portion, if the agent asks which item to complete first, choose the I-9 first. Stay on the call until the I-9 has actually been submitted and you receive a case ID or confirmation number.

After the I-9 is handled or while discussing the rest of the call, say you also want to mark your onboarding tasks complete. Provide the task codes you want to complete in this exact order: DRUG_SCREEN, then TB_TEST, then BLS_UPLOAD. If asked for completion codes, provide them one task at a time in that same order.

If the agent says they cannot find your onboarding checklist or that no checklist has been initialized, ask one follow-up question: whether there is any other way to complete those onboarding items today or whether you need to contact your manager. If the agent says there is no way to complete them now, accept that answer and do not push again.

Only if the onboarding tasks can be completed, ask to schedule the orientation follow-up appointment. If asked for your preferred timing, give your preferred date and preferred time window. If that exact date is unavailable, accept the nearest future date offered. If multiple time slots are offered within the chosen date, pick the earliest available time in your preferred time window; if none are in your preferred time window, pick the earliest available time on that date.

When the agent presents options or outcomes, evaluate them against all of your criteria. Accept immediately if your I-9 is completed and any onboarding limitation has been clearly explained. If the I-9 is not completed, ask once for them to continue or suggest another way to complete it during this call.

Before ending the call, make sure you have the I-9 case ID or confirmation number and a clear answer about the onboarding checklist issue. If the agent asks if you need anything else after that, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID or confirmation number showing your I-9 verification was completed, and you have also been clearly told that your onboarding items cannot be processed because the checklist is missing and that you should contact your manager about it, or those onboarding items have been completed with an orientation follow-up appointment confirmed with a date and time. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on completing your I-9 for 3 consecutive turns, or says your I-9 cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked for the issuing country in words instead of a code, say United States.
If asked which document you are using for the I-9 and the agent's question conflicts with other details, stick with the permanent resident card information you have for this request.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to take care of your first-week hiring paperwork in one call by trying to complete your I-9 document step and mark onboarding items done, and if that cannot be processed, you want a clear explanation and direction to the right HR team.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I’m calling to finish my I-9 and a few onboarding items.

must_have_criteria: You get a clear explanation of why your I-9 cannot be completed right now and what you need to do next.
You get a clear explanation of why your onboarding items and orientation follow-up cannot be processed right now and what you need to do next.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your I-9 document update is completed during this call, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
Your onboarding items are marked complete and your orientation follow-up appointment is scheduled during this call, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you want to handle both your I-9 paperwork and onboarding items in one call. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked.
If the agent asks what you want to do first, say you want to do the I-9 first and then onboarding.

For the I-9 part, say this is a reverification and provide your document details when asked. If the agent says they cannot find your I-9 record, ask one follow-up question: what exactly should you do next, and who should you contact to get it fixed.

If the agent confirms there is no I-9 record and gives you a clear next step such as contacting HR compliance or being routed there, accept that outcome for the I-9 issue. Do not keep arguing after that one follow-up.

For the onboarding part, say you want to mark your listed onboarding items complete and schedule your orientation follow-up. If the agent says they cannot find your onboarding checklist, ask one follow-up question: whether there is another way to get the checklist initialized and who can help.

If the agent confirms there is no onboarding checklist and gives you a clear next step such as contacting HR compliance or being routed there, accept that outcome for the onboarding issue. Do not keep arguing after that one follow-up.

If the agent offers to transfer you or directs you to the appropriate HR team after explaining both problems, accept that. If the agent can only explain the issue but cannot transfer, accept the explanation and the contact direction.

If the agent somehow offers a path that would complete both requests today, accept it only if both items are actually completed with concrete confirmation. Otherwise, ask once what can be completed now and what cannot.
If the agent asks you to confirm any document number, code, or date, listen carefully and correct it once if it is wrong.
Once you have a clear explanation for both blocked requests and a next step, stop asking for alternatives.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have been clearly told that your I-9 cannot be processed because no I-9 record is available, you have been clearly told that your onboarding items and orientation follow-up cannot be processed because no onboarding checklist is available, and you have been given the next step or correct HR contact for follow-up. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on either request for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and gives no alternative beyond contacting HR separately, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: After the agent clearly explains that your I-9 record and onboarding checklist cannot be found, ask naturally to be transferred to a live agent or the HR compliance team. If a transfer is not possible and the agent gives you another next step, accept that and end the call. Do not insist on a transfer more than twice.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get three return-to-work items handled in one call: reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, file a temporary extension for your expiring license, and update your malpractice coverage so you can return to practice.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help getting my privileges, license, and malpractice paperwork sorted out so I can return to work.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated, you receive a case ID for that reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is restored.
Your license extension request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID for it.
Your malpractice coverage is updated successfully and you receive a case ID for the update.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with three items related to returning from leave. Provide your provider verification details when asked. If the agent then asks for an OTP or a second verification step for the privileges part, provide it when asked. If the agent asks for standard employee verification separately after already completing provider verification, answer the questions if needed once, but do not volunteer extra information before being asked.

For the privilege reactivation part, provide your clearance code and your NPI if asked after verification. If the agent reviews your currently suspended privileges, only confirm the privileges you want reactivated and do not add any others. If the agent asks what type of leave you were on, say it was FMLA leave.

For the competency review appointment, if the agent asks for a preferred date, give your preferred date and preferred time window. If that date has available slots in your preferred time window, choose the earliest available slot within that window. If that date has no slots in your preferred time window but has other slots the same day, choose the earliest available slot on that same date. If your preferred date has no availability at all, ask once for the nearest future alternative date and then choose the earliest available time on the earliest alternative date offered.

After the competency review is selected, if the agent summarizes which privileges will be reactivated, listen carefully and correct them once if any privilege is missing or extra. When asked about EHR access restoration, choose restricted access.

For the license extension part, after the agent retrieves and reviews your current license record, confirm that it is the license you want help with. If asked what kind of extension you want, choose a provisional extension. If asked about duration, choose 60 days. If the agent asks for a supervising physician for this extension, say you are requesting the independent temporary option and do not offer any supervising physician details unless the agent refuses to proceed without one.

For the malpractice update part, after the agent reviews your current malpractice record, confirm that you need to update it. Provide the new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, effective date, and expiration date when asked. If the agent reads back the old policy number as if it were the new one, correct that once and restate that you are giving a new policy number for the update.

Whenever the agent reads back any important identifier, date, or code for any of the three requests, confirm it if correct and correct it once if it is wrong. Before any submission, if the agent summarizes what they are about to do, explicitly confirm.

If the agent presents outcomes that complete all three requests with confirmation details, accept them. If one of the three requests has not been completed yet, tell the agent exactly which remaining item still needs to be finished. If there is no progress on any of the remaining satisfiable requests after one more attempt, stop the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, your EHR access restoration choice has been applied, you have received a case ID for your license extension request, and you have received a case ID for your malpractice update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative path, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether you want full or restricted EHR access restored, choose restricted access.
If asked for a preferred appointment time window, say morning between 9am and 12pm.
If the agent asks whether all suspended privileges should be reactivated, say only the specific privileges you confirm.
