user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get three return-to-work items handled in one call: reactivate your suspended clinical privileges, file a temporary extension for your expiring license, and update your malpractice coverage with your new carrier.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help getting my privileges, license, and malpractice paperwork updated so I can return to work.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated for the specific suspended privileges you confirm, you receive a case ID for that reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is restored.
Your license extension request is submitted and you receive a case ID for it.
Your malpractice coverage is updated to your new policy and you receive a case ID for that update.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you need help with three return-to-work items. When asked to verify your identity, provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent then needs a second verification step for the privileges request, provide your OTP code when asked after confirming the phone number on file if the agent asks about it.

After verification, if the agent asks what you need, clearly say you want to handle privilege reactivation first, then your license extension, then your malpractice update. If the agent can only process them one at a time, agree to that order.

For the privilege reactivation portion, provide your NPI again only if the agent asks for it after the higher-security verification. Provide your occupational health clearance code when asked. When the agent reviews your suspended privileges, confirm only the privileges you want restored. If the agent asks what type of leave you were on, say it was medical leave.

For the competency review appointment, if the agent asks for a preferred date, give your preferred date and time window. If that exact date has available slots in your preferred time window, choose the earliest available slot within that time window. If that date has no slots in your preferred time window but has other slots that day, ask once whether there is anything closer to the middle of the day; if not, accept the earliest available slot on that same date. If your preferred date has no availability at all, accept the nearest future alternative date offered and choose the earliest available time on that date.

Before the privilege reactivation is submitted, listen to the summary and confirm it if it matches what you asked for. After the reactivation is completed, if the agent asks what level of EHR access you need restored, choose full access.

For the license extension portion, provide your state license number when asked and confirm it when the agent reads it back. When asked what kind of extension you want, say you want a provisional extension. If asked for the length, choose 60 days. If the agent offers only allowed duration choices, keep 60 days. If the agent says the request is eligible, confirm the submission when they summarize it.

For the malpractice update portion, after the agent reviews your current record, provide your new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. Make sure the agent is treating the new policy number as different from your current one on file. When the agent reads the details back, confirm them if correct and authorize the update.

At every read-back of any identifier, code, date, or policy number, listen carefully. If the agent says any value incorrectly, correct it once immediately. If the corrected read-back is accurate, confirm and proceed.
Do not end the call after one item is finished. After each completed item, remind the agent that you still need the remaining unresolved items handled in the same call.
Once all three items are completed, listen for the case IDs and the confirmed appointment date and time. If the agent asks whether you need anything else, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, your EHR access has been restored, you have received a case ID for your license extension request, and you have received a case ID for your malpractice coverage update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or clearly says one of these requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative path forward, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether you want full or restricted EHR access restored, choose full access.
If asked whether your leave was medical, personal, or FMLA, say medical leave.
If the agent gives a wrong privilege code, license number, policy number, or date, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get three return-to-work issues handled in one call: try to reactivate your clinical privileges, file a temporary extension for your license, and update your malpractice coverage.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help clearing a few return-to-work items.

must_have_criteria: Your license extension request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your malpractice coverage update is completed successfully and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want your clinical privileges reactivated so you can return to practice, but if you are told this cannot be processed because your clearance is not valid, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you need help with three things: privilege reactivation, a license extension, and a malpractice update. Provide your authentication details when asked. If asked for one item at a time, keep the same order: privileges first, then license, then malpractice.

For authentication, provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked. If the agent then requires an extra text-message code because of the privileges request, provide your employee ID only if the agent already has it from your provider verification or asks to confirm the phone number, then give the OTP code when asked.

For the privilege reactivation part, provide your employee ID and date of birth only if specifically requested after the provider verification path does not cover it, then provide your NPI, your clearance code, and confirm only the privilege codes you want reactivated. If appointment scheduling is offered before reactivation, ask for your preferred date and choose the earliest available time within your preferred time window. If your preferred date has no availability, accept the nearest future date offered and choose the earliest available time within your preferred time window if one exists; otherwise choose the earliest available slot on that date.

If the agent says your privilege reactivation cannot go through because your clearance code is invalid or does not match, ask once if there is any other way to proceed today or if an updated clearance from occupational health is required. If the agent clearly says you need a current clearance or need to visit occupational health, accept that outcome and move on to the license extension without asking again.

For the license extension part, provide your state license number when asked and confirm you want a supervised extension. If asked about duration, choose 30 days. If asked for a supervising physician, provide the supervising physician's NPI and listen for the read-back; correct any mistake once. If the agent offers different valid extension lengths, stay with 30 days.

Before the license extension is submitted, listen to the summary carefully. If any identifier, extension type, supervising physician NPI, or duration is wrong, correct it once. When the summary matches what you want, explicitly confirm the submission and wait for a case ID.

For the malpractice update part, after the license extension is handled, provide the new carrier, the new policy number, the per-occurrence limit, the aggregate limit, and the effective and expiration dates. If the agent reads back the existing policy number instead of the new one, correct that once and insist they use the new policy number for the update.

When the agent summarizes the malpractice update, confirm only if the carrier, new policy number, both coverage limits, and both dates are correct. Then wait for the update to be completed and for a case ID.

When the agent presents options, evaluate them against all criteria. Accept any path that completes both the license extension and malpractice update, even if the privilege reactivation is denied because your clearance is invalid. If the agent offers only partial progress on a satisfiable item, ask once what is still needed to finish it during this call.

At the end, make sure you have a case ID for the license extension, a case ID for the malpractice update, and a clear statement that the privilege reactivation could not be completed because your clearance is not valid and you need updated clearance. Once you have all three outcomes, end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your license extension, a case ID for your malpractice coverage update, and a clear explanation that your privilege reactivation could not be completed because your clearance code is not valid and you need updated occupational health clearance. decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your satisfiable requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the license extension or malpractice update cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or go to occupational health for the privileges issue, accept that and end the call once the other completed items are wrapped up.

If asked what EHR access you need after privilege reactivation, choose restricted access.
If the agent gives a wrong read-back for any code, number, or date, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get everything in place so you can start work at your new location: reactivate your clinical privileges after returning from leave, transfer your DEA registration to the new facility in the new state, and update your malpractice coverage.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help reactivating my privileges and updating my records for a move to a new facility.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated, you receive a case ID for that reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is restored.
Your DEA registration transfer is completed and you receive a case ID for the transfer.
Your malpractice coverage is updated and you receive a case ID for the update.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you need three things handled in this call: privilege reactivation after leave, DEA transfer to a new facility in another state, and a malpractice insurance update. Provide your authentication details when asked. Because one of your requests involves DEA transfer, provide the OTP code when asked after the base verification is completed.

If the agent asks for details one request at a time, cooperate and stay organized. Do not introduce extra requests. If the agent asks you to repeat information already given, repeat it once without arguing.

For the privilege reactivation part, provide your NPI after OTP if asked, then provide your occupational health clearance code, confirm that you only want the listed suspended privileges reactivated, and identify the leave type you were on. If the agent offers appointment times for the competency review on your preferred date, accept the earliest available time slot within your preferred time window. If your preferred date has no availability, accept the nearest future alternative date offered and choose the earliest available time slot within your preferred time window on that date. If no slot is offered within your preferred time window on the chosen date, accept the earliest available slot on that date.

For the DEA transfer part, provide your DEA number, confirm the current DEA record when it is read back, and then give the new facility, new state, transfer reason, and effective date. If the agent restates the new state as a full name or abbreviation, accept either as long as it clearly matches your intended state. If the agent asks whether you are moving permanently or adding a site, stick to your stated reason and do not change it.

For the malpractice update part, confirm the current malpractice record when it is reviewed with you, then provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent reads back any number or date incorrectly, correct it once and wait for the corrected read-back before agreeing.

When the agent summarizes any submission before finalizing it, listen carefully and only confirm if the summary matches what you asked for. If something is wrong, correct the mismatch once and ask them to read it back again.

When the agent presents outcomes, evaluate all three requests together. If all three are completed with the required confirmations, accept the result. If one or two are completed but one is still unresolved, keep the call focused on finishing the unresolved must-have request. If the agent says a satisfiable request cannot be completed but offers no concrete alternative, ask once for them to check again or suggest the next available way to complete it during this call. If there is still no progress after that, stop.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, your EHR access update has been confirmed, you have received a case ID for your DEA transfer, and you have received a case ID for your malpractice update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says one of these requests cannot be processed and gives no alternative that moves it forward, say goodbye and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to get three things handled in one call before you can start at your new location: reactivate your clinical privileges after leave, transfer your DEA registration to the new facility in a different state, and update your malpractice coverage.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help getting my privileges, DEA, and malpractice updated before I start at a new site.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated, you receive a case ID for that reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and your EHR access is restored.
Your DEA transfer to your new facility is submitted and you receive a case ID for it.
Your malpractice coverage is updated and you receive a case ID for that update.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly stating that you need help with three items for your return to work and transfer. If the agent asks for verification, provide your NPI, facility code, and PIN. If the agent then asks for an OTP because of the DEA transfer, confirm the last four digits of the phone number only if you recognize them, then provide the OTP code when asked. Do not offer extra details before verification is complete.

If the agent asks what you need after verification, state all three requests at a high level: privilege reactivation after leave, DEA transfer to a new facility in another state, and malpractice coverage update.

For the privilege reactivation portion, provide your NPI when asked after OTP if needed for that part, then provide your occupational health clearance code and confirm the specific suspended privileges you want reactivated. If asked what kind of leave you were on, say FMLA leave.

For the competency review appointment, if the agent asks for your preferred date, give your preferred date and say you prefer a morning time window. If that date has available slots, accept the earliest available time in the morning. If there are no slots on that date, ask once for any other available dates, then accept the earliest alternative date offered and choose the available time closest to 10:00 AM.

Before the privilege reactivation is submitted, listen to the read-back carefully. If any privilege code, clearance code, leave type, or appointment date and time is wrong, correct it once. When asked about EHR access after reactivation, choose full access restoration.

For the DEA transfer portion, provide your DEA number when asked. After the current DEA record is reviewed with you, provide the new facility code, the new state, the reason for the transfer, and the effective date. If the agent says the state must be abbreviated, use the two-letter code. If the agent reads back any DEA number, facility code, state, reason, or effective date incorrectly, correct it once before confirming.

For the malpractice update portion, after the current record is reviewed, provide the new carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date. If the agent reads back either coverage amount or either date incorrectly, correct it once before confirming. Since all nice-to-have criteria are empty, accept the completed update as soon as it meets the must-have outcome.

Whenever the agent presents a final confirmation for any of the three requests, evaluate whether that request has actually been completed and whether you received the needed evidence, such as a case ID or confirmed appointment time. If it has, accept it and move to the next unresolved request.

If the agent offers an option that does not complete one of your required requests, tell them clearly which unresolved item still needs to be completed and ask them to continue once. If there is still no progress after that, follow the failure condition.
At the end, make sure all three requests have been completed. If the agent asks whether you need anything else after all three are done, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a specific date and time, your EHR access restoration has been confirmed, you have received a case ID for your DEA transfer, and you have received a case ID for your malpractice update. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says any required part cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent asks whether you want full or restricted EHR access restored, choose full access.
If the agent asks for the new state in words instead of a code, say Colorado.
If the agent gives a wrong identifier, code, or date, correct it once.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to get cleared to start work at your new location by having your suspended clinical privileges reactivated, your DEA registration transferred to the new facility, and your malpractice coverage updated if possible.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help getting set up to start at a new facility.

must_have_criteria: Your clinical privileges are reactivated, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, and you receive a case ID for the reactivation.
Your DEA registration transfer is completed and you receive a case ID for the transfer.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your malpractice coverage is updated during this call, but if you are told this is not possible because there is no malpractice record on file, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you are returning from leave, moving to a new facility, and need help with privileges, DEA, and malpractice. When asked for verification, provide your provider details first. If the agent then needs a texted code for the DEA-related part of the call, provide the last four digits confirmation if the number sounds right and then provide the OTP code when asked.

For your privilege reactivation request, after any required verification is complete, provide your NPI and clearance code when asked. If the agent reviews your suspended privileges, confirm only the privileges you want reactivated. Your selection rule is: if both suspended privileges are offered, confirm both; if only one of them is listed, confirm that one only and do not add anything else.

For the competency review appointment, if asked for your preferred timing, state your preferred date and time window. If slots are offered on your preferred date, choose the earliest available slot within your preferred time window. If nothing is available in that window on your preferred date, choose the earliest available slot on that same date. If your preferred date has no availability at all, accept the earliest alternative date offered and choose the earliest available time on that date.

If the agent asks what kind of leave you were on for the privilege reactivation, identify it as the leave type you were on. If the agent asks what kind of EHR access you want restored after reactivation, choose full access restored.

For your DEA transfer request, provide your DEA number when asked and listen for the current DEA details to be reviewed back to you. Then provide the new facility, the new state, the reason for the transfer, and the effective date. If the agent offers to use the facility from your verification instead of the new one, correct them once and make clear the transfer is for the new facility, not your current home facility.

For the state on the DEA transfer, if the agent asks for the state in words, say Georgia. If the agent asks for an abbreviation, say GA. Before the transfer is submitted, listen to the read-back and correct any wrong DEA number, facility code, state, or effective date once.

For your malpractice update request, ask to update that coverage as part of the same call. If the agent says they cannot update it because there is no malpractice record on file, ask one follow-up question: what you should do next to get it set up. After they clearly explain that this part cannot be completed now and direct you to credentialing or the appropriate next step, accept that outcome and move on. Do not argue further.

When the agent presents options or outcomes across the call, evaluate them against all of your goals. Accept any path that completes both the privilege reactivation and the DEA transfer, even if the malpractice update cannot be completed. If the agent offers alternatives that would delay or avoid completing one of those two successful requests during this call, ask once if they can still finish them now. If they can, continue. If they cannot make progress after that, stop.

At the end, make sure you have the reactivation case ID, the confirmed competency review appointment date and time, the DEA transfer case ID, and a clear explanation of what to do about the malpractice issue. Once you have all of that, do not raise any new requests.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your privilege reactivation, your competency review appointment is confirmed with a date and time, your EHR access restoration has been completed, you have received a case ID for your DEA transfer, and you have been clearly told that the malpractice update cannot be completed in this call because no malpractice record exists on file and that you need to contact credentialing to initialize it. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your privilege reactivation or DEA transfer for 3 consecutive turns, or if the agent says those requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If the agent gives a wrong privilege code, DEA number, facility code, state, or date, correct it once.
If the agent asks for the state as a full name instead of a code, say Georgia.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to handle your move to a new facility by transferring your DEA registration, updating your malpractice coverage for the new location, and submitting a license extension request so you can keep working while your renewal is processed.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a few provider credentialing updates related to my move.

must_have_criteria: Your DEA transfer is submitted and you receive a case ID.
Your malpractice coverage update is completed and you receive a case ID.
Your license extension request is submitted and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you are calling about three provider updates tied to your relocation. Provide your NPI, home facility code, and PIN when asked. If asked to complete a text-message verification for the DEA part, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the code.
If any identifier is read back to you during verification or record lookup, listen carefully and correct it once if it is wrong. Do not volunteer extra details before the agent asks.

For the DEA transfer, provide your DEA number first when asked. After the agent reviews the current DEA record with you, confirm that you want it transferred to your new facility in the new state for your stated move-related reason, effective on your specified date. If the agent gives you options or asks for clarification, accept the transfer details exactly as planned and do not change them.

If the agent asks for the new state in words or abbreviation, use the state abbreviation. If the agent reads back the new facility, state, reason, or effective date incorrectly, correct the mistake once before the transfer is submitted.

For the malpractice update, wait for the agent to review your current policy with you first. Then provide the new carrier, new policy number, coverage limits, and policy dates. If the agent presents the old policy number as if it were the new one, correct them and restate that you are updating to a different policy number.

When the agent summarizes the malpractice update before submitting it, confirm only if the carrier, new policy number, per-occurrence limit, aggregate limit, effective date, and expiration date all match what you provided. If any one item is wrong, correct it once and ask them to read it back again.

For the license extension, wait for the agent to review your current license record and confirm that an extension is possible. Then say you need an extension while your renewal is still being processed. If asked which type of extension you want, choose the independent option that does not involve a supervising physician. If asked about duration, choose the exact duration you planned.

If the agent offers only the allowed duration choices, accept your planned duration immediately. If they misstate your license number, extension type, or duration in the final read-back, correct it once before approving submission.

Whenever the agent presents a final summary before submitting any of the three requests, evaluate it against all of your goals. If it fully matches the request being handled, approve it. If something is missing or wrong, ask for one correction and one new read-back. If there is still no progress after that, move on only if your other unresolved must-have requests are still being actively worked.

Do not end the call after the first or second request is completed. Stay on the line until all three requests have either been completed with case IDs or the agent has clearly stopped making progress for 3 consecutive turns.
At the end, make sure you have heard a case ID for each completed request. If the agent asks whether you need anything else after all three are done, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your DEA transfer, a case ID for your malpractice coverage update, and a case ID for your license extension request. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative next step, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to add your new baby as a dependent on your visa petition, open FMLA bonding leave, and request a few PTO days right before that leave starts.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with my visa paperwork, leave, and some PTO.

must_have_criteria: Your visa dependent addition is completed and you receive a confirmation or amendment ID.
Your FMLA leave is opened and you receive an FMLA case ID, and your return-to-work check-in is scheduled with a confirmed date and time.
Your PTO request is submitted and you receive a case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you have three things to do in one call: add a dependent to your visa petition, file leave, and request PTO. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked to complete a text-message verification, provide the last four digits of your phone number if you recognize them, then provide the OTP code from the text message.

For the visa dependent request, provide your visa petition number when asked. Then provide your dependent's first and last name, spelling each name if the agent asks. Provide the relationship, date of birth, country of birth, and the USCIS receipt number. If the agent reads back either number incorrectly or mixes the two numbers up, correct it once and ask them to use the corrected value.

Accept the visa dependent portion only after the dependent has actually been added and you receive a confirmation or amendment ID. If the agent has not completed it yet, ask them to finish that request before moving on.

For the FMLA request, say this leave is for bonding with your new child. Provide the leave dates when asked. If the agent asks for a preferred date for the return-to-work check-in, give your preferred date and preferred time window. If that date is unavailable, accept the nearest future date the agent offers. If multiple times are offered on the accepted date, choose the earliest time within your preferred time window; if none fall in your preferred time window, choose the earliest available time on that date.

Accept the FMLA portion only after the leave case has been opened, you receive the case ID, and the return-to-work check-in is confirmed with a specific date and time. If the agent discusses the leave but has not actually opened it, ask them to submit it.

For the PTO request, provide the PTO date range and confirm that you want to use general PTO, not sick leave. When the agent tells you how many days will count and be deducted, listen to the counted dates and confirm the request if they match your range. If the agent says some dates do not count, accept that calculation as long as the request itself can still be submitted.

Accept the PTO portion only after the request has actually been submitted and you receive a case ID with the deducted days confirmed. If the agent is only describing the balance or eligibility, ask them to complete the submission.

Throughout the call, when the agent presents options, evaluate them against all of your goals. If an option completes all remaining requests, accept it. If the agent can only complete some of the requests at that moment, let them finish each satisfiable request one by one, but stay on the call until all three are completed.

Before the call ends, make sure each completed request has its own concrete outcome: a confirmation or amendment ID for the visa update, a case ID and appointment for the leave, and a case ID with deducted days for the PTO. If any one of those is missing, ask for that missing item once.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a confirmation or amendment ID for your visa dependent addition, an FMLA case ID and a confirmed return-to-work check-in appointment with a date and time, and a case ID for your PTO request with the deducted days confirmed. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your three requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says the requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the PTO should be general or sick leave, choose general PTO.
If asked for your dependent's country of birth in words instead of a code, say United Kingdom.
If the agent gives a leave category that is not bonding, correct it once and say the leave is for bonding with your new child.
If the agent gives a return-to-work appointment before your leave end date, reject that appointment and ask for one on or after your leave end date.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to add your new baby as a dependent on your visa petition, ask about opening bonding leave, and submit a short sick leave request for the days right before that leave starts.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with my visa paperwork, leave, and a few days off.

must_have_criteria: Your visa dependent addition is completed and you receive a confirmation or amendment ID.
Your PTO request is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID with the deducted days confirmed.
nice_to_have_criteria: You want to open your bonding leave request, but if you are told this is not possible, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you have three things to handle: adding a dependent to your visa petition, asking about leave for bonding with your baby, and requesting a few sick days before that. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If a texted code is required, confirm the phone number if you recognize it and then provide the OTP code.

For the visa dependent request, provide your visa petition number when asked and let the agent read it back. Then provide your dependent's first and last name with spelling, relationship, date of birth, country of birth, and the USCIS receipt number. If the agent asks for the country in words instead of a code, say Philippines. If the agent reads back any of these details incorrectly, correct them once. Stay on this topic until the dependent addition is either completed with a confirmation or clearly blocked.

For the leave request, explain that you want bonding leave for the stated date range. If the agent says they need to check whether you qualify, let them do that before giving more detail. If they say you are not eligible, ask one follow-up question: why not, and is there any way to still file it now? If they clearly say no, accept that outcome and move on. Do not argue more than that.

For the PTO request, give the stated date range and, if asked to choose between general PTO or sick leave, choose sick leave. If the agent tells you how many days will be deducted and which dates count, listen carefully and confirm as long as the request can still be submitted for the dates you asked for. If there is a problem with those dates, ask one time whether there is any nearby alternative that would work; otherwise accept the best option that still gets your PTO submitted.

When the agent presents options for any part of the call, first check whether they still allow both your visa dependent addition and your PTO request to be completed. If yes, but your leave request cannot be completed, ask once whether there is any other way to handle that leave request. If the answer is no, stop pushing and continue with the other items.

Before any final submission, if the agent summarizes details back to you, confirm them if they match what you intended. If they do not match, correct the mistake once and ask them to use the corrected information.
Do not introduce any new requests. Once you have a completed visa dependent addition, a completed PTO request with a case ID, and a clear answer about the leave request, end the call.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a confirmation or amendment ID for the visa dependent addition, a case ID for your PTO request with the deducted days confirmed, and a clear explanation that your bonding leave request cannot be filed at this time. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your satisfiable requests for 3 consecutive turns, or says your requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the PTO should be general or sick leave, choose sick leave.
If asked for your dependent's country of birth in words instead of a code, say Philippines.
If the agent gives a wrong date range or wrong dependent detail, correct it once.

negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you need help with leave, a shift swap, and sick time. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If asked for a one-time code, provide it. If the agent asks which issue to handle first, say you want to start with the leave request.

For the leave request, provide your leave category and the leave start and end dates only after the agent is ready for those details. If the agent offers appointment times for your return-to-work check-in on your preferred date, accept the earliest available time within your preferred time window. If your preferred date has no availability, accept the nearest future date the agent offers and choose the earliest available time within your preferred time window on that date.

If the agent gives you a summary of the leave request before submitting it, listen carefully and confirm it if the details match what you want. Do not move on from this part until the leave has actually been submitted and you are given a case ID plus a confirmed appointment date and time.

For the shift swap, provide the shift ID and your colleague's employee ID when asked. If the agent reads either identifier back incorrectly, correct it once. If the swap is eligible, confirm that you want to proceed. If the agent says there is a problem and offers an alternative that still results in the swap being completed for that same shift with that same colleague, accept it; otherwise ask once for them to check again or explain the issue.

Do not accept the shift-swap portion as finished until the swap has actually been confirmed and you are given a case ID. If the agent clearly says the swap cannot be completed and there is no alternative after one follow-up, stop pursuing that part and continue only if the other requests are still moving forward.

For the sick leave request, provide the requested date range and confirm that you want to use sick leave if asked. When the agent tells you how many days will be deducted and which dates count, accept it if it covers your requested date range. If the agent offers multiple valid options for dates, choose the option that starts on your requested start date; if that is not available, choose the option with the earliest start date.

If the agent summarizes the sick leave request before submitting it, confirm it if the dates and leave type are correct. Do not accept this part as complete until the request has actually been submitted and you are given a case ID.

Whenever the agent presents options, first check whether they still allow all three of your requests to be completed. If an option would prevent one of your requests from being completed, say what is still missing and ask once for another option or for the agent to continue with the remaining requests.

At the end, listen for all confirmation numbers and the scheduled return-to-work check-in date and time. If any one of the three requests is still missing a completed outcome, ask specifically for that missing item once before ending the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to file FMLA leave for your upcoming treatment and recovery period, swap a shift you can no longer work before that leave starts, and also see whether you can use sick time for a few days before the procedure.

must_have_criteria: Your FMLA leave is opened and you receive a case ID.
Your shift swap is confirmed and you receive a case ID.
nice_to_have_criteria: Your sick leave request is approved for the days you want off, but if you are told this is not possible because you do not have enough balance, accept that outcome.
negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you need help with leave, a shift swap, and sick time. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. Because your leave request is sensitive, also provide the OTP code when asked after the text message step.

For any choice or read-back the agent gives you, evaluate all three goals together: you must get the leave case opened and the shift swap confirmed, and you would also like the sick leave approved if possible.

Handle the FMLA request first if the agent is ready to do that. If the agent asks for details, say the leave is for your military-related need category and give your leave dates. If the agent needs to schedule a return-to-work check-in, give your preferred appointment date and preferred time window.

If the agent offers appointment times on your preferred date, accept the earliest available time within your preferred time window. If nothing is available on that date, accept the earliest time on the earliest alternative future date the agent offers.
If the agent reads back your leave dates, category, or appointment details and anything is wrong, correct it once. If the read-back is correct, explicitly confirm they should submit it.

For the shift swap, provide the shift ID and your colleague's employee ID when asked. If the agent confirms the colleague is eligible, explicitly agree to proceed with the swap. If the agent reads back either employee ID or the shift ID incorrectly, correct it once before they continue.

For the sick leave request, provide the requested date range and say you want to use sick leave. If the agent tells you how many days you currently have available and says the request cannot be approved because your balance is too low, ask once whether there is any other way to approve at least those same requested days.
If the agent says there are no other options for the sick leave request, accept that outcome and do not ask again. Continue with the leave case and shift swap if those are still in progress.
If the agent presents an option that completes both your leave case and your shift swap, accept it even if the sick leave request is denied.

If the agent cannot move either the leave case or the shift swap forward, tell them you need those two items completed and ask them to check again or suggest an alternative once. If there is still no progress after that, stop.
Before the call ends, listen for the confirmation numbers or case IDs. If one of your completed requests is missing a case ID, ask for the confirmation number for that completed request once.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received an FMLA case ID, your return-to-work check-in appointment is confirmed with a date and time, you have received a case ID confirming the shift swap, and you have been clearly told that your sick leave request cannot be approved because of insufficient balance after being told your current balance. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on your leave case or shift swap for 3 consecutive turns, or says those requests cannot be processed and offers no alternative, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked whether the time off before your procedure should be general PTO or sick leave, choose sick leave.
If asked for a preferred appointment time window, say morning.
If the agent gives a wrong leave date range, PTO date range, shift ID, or colleague employee ID, correct it once.

negotiation_behavior: Start by briefly saying you have three things to do today: onboarding, I-9, and PTO. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If the agent asks what you want to handle first, say you are fine doing them in any order.

For the onboarding part, provide the task codes you want marked complete only when asked. If the agent asks for completion codes, give the code for each task one at a time in the same order the agent asks for them. If appointment slots are offered on your preferred date, choose the earliest available time that falls in the morning. If there are no slots on your preferred date, ask once for the nearest future date with availability and then accept the earliest available time on that nearest date.

For the I-9 part, provide the document details only when asked. If the agent asks you to choose between document list options, choose the single-document option you already have. When the agent tells you how many days will be deducted and which dates count, accept only if the request covers your full requested range and the deducted days are explicitly confirmed before submission.

Whenever the agent presents options, evaluate them against all of your goals for that part of the call. If an option fully completes the current item, accept it. If it does not complete the current item, ask once what is still needed. If there is still no progress after that, stop pursuing that part and move on only if the other parts can still be completed.

Before any final submission or booking, if the agent summarizes what they are about to do, confirm it if it matches your request. If any date, code, or document detail is wrong, correct it once before agreeing.
Do not end the call until all three items have been completed with clear confirmation. At the end, listen for the confirmation numbers and appointment details, then say thank you and end the call.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to take care of three HR items in one call: correct a recent timesheet issue, register your upcoming on-call availability, and swap an upcoming shift with a colleague.
user_goal.starting_utterance: I need help with a payroll correction, an on-call registration, and a shift swap.

must_have_criteria: Your payroll correction is submitted successfully and you receive a case ID.
Your on-call availability is registered successfully and you receive a registration ID.
Your shift swap is confirmed successfully and you receive a case ID.
negotiation_behavior: Start by stating that you have three items to handle in one call. Provide your employee ID and date of birth when asked. If the agent asks which issue to handle first, say you are fine doing them in any order.

For the payroll correction, give the shift ID for the timesheet issue when asked. If the agent reviews the current record, listen and confirm it is the correct shift. If asked what needs to be corrected, say the differential was missed and give the correct total hours worked for that shift. If the agent summarizes the correction before submitting it, confirm it. Stay on this item until you receive a case ID or the agent clearly says it cannot be processed.

If the agent says the payroll correction cannot be processed, ask once why not and whether there is any alternative. If there is still no way to complete it, do not keep arguing; move on to the next request.

For the on-call registration, give the unit first if asked, then provide your availability start date and end date. If asked whether you are signing up as primary or backup, choose primary. If asked about blackout dates, say you do not have any. If the agent reviews any existing on-call registrations or eligibility details, confirm you still want to register this new window. If the agent summarizes the registration details before submitting, confirm them. Stay on this item until you receive a registration ID or the agent clearly says it cannot be processed.

If the agent says the on-call registration cannot be completed, ask once what is preventing it and whether there is another way to register for that same window. If there is still no way to complete it, accept that outcome and move on to the next request.

For the shift swap, provide the shift ID for the upcoming shift when asked. If the agent reviews the shift details, confirm it is the correct shift. When asked who you want to swap with, provide your colleague's employee ID. If the agent says your colleague is eligible and summarizes the swap before submitting it, confirm it. Stay on this item until you receive a case ID or the agent clearly says it cannot be processed.

If the agent says the shift cannot be swapped, ask once whether there are any alternatives or what specifically is blocking it. If there is still no way to complete the swap, accept that outcome and stop pushing.

Whenever the agent presents a summary for any of the three requests, check that it matches the request you made. If a date, shift ID, hours value, unit, colleague ID, or on-call window is wrong, correct it once clearly before confirming.
If the agent completes one or two of the requests first, continue with the remaining request or requests until each one has either been completed with a confirmation ID or clearly denied.
After all three requests have been addressed, listen for the confirmation numbers and brief summary. If the agent asks whether you need anything else, say no.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: You have received a case ID for your payroll correction, a registration ID for your on-call availability, and a case ID confirming your shift swap. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent makes no progress on any of your satisfiable requests for 3 consecutive turns, say goodbye and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent cannot help after 3 consecutive turns without progress, say goodbye and end the call. If the agent tells you that you need to visit HR in person or call back later, accept that and end the call.
edge_cases: If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.
If told to visit HR in person for any part of this request, accept that and end the call.
Do not request services beyond your stated HR request.
If asked which correction type applies to the payroll issue, choose missed differential pay.
If the agent gives a wrong shift ID, unit, date range, colleague employee ID, or hours amount, correct it once.
If asked whether you have blackout dates for the on-call window, say no.
